howItWorks.intro: This import tariff calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Additional Tariff = Goods Value × Additional Rate%
Landed Cost = CIF + All Duties + MPF + HMF
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is CIF value?
faq.answer: CIF (Cost, Insurance, and Freight) is the customs value used to calculate duties. It includes the goods value (FOB), shipping/freight costs, and insurance. Most countries base duties on CIF value.

faq.question: What are Section 301 tariffs?
faq.answer: Section 301 tariffs are additional duties imposed by the US on specific goods from countries found to have unfair trade practices. Currently, many Chinese goods face 7.5-25% additional tariffs on top of regular duties.

faq.question: What is the Merchandise Processing Fee?
faq.answer: The MPF is a US Customs fee of 0.3464% of the CIF value, with a minimum of $31.67 and maximum of $614.35 per entry. It applies to most formal entries.

faq.question: Is the Import Tariff Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Import Tariff Calculator?
sections.body: The Import Tariff Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your goods value, freight/shipping cost, insurance, you get instant results including cif value, base duty amount, additional tariff amount. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting cif value right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your goods value and need to find the right cif value. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Goods Value (FOB) ($)

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: freight/shipping cost, insurance, duty rate, additional tariff. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your cif value, base duty amount, additional tariff amount. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your goods value before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
